lines.gurmukhi: ਗੁਰ ਸੇਵਾ ਸਦਾ ਸੁਖੁ ਹੈ ਜਿਸ ਨੋ ਹੁਕਮੁ ਮਨਾਏ ॥੭॥
lines.translit: gur sevaa sadaa sukh hai jis no hukam manaae |7|
lines.english: Serving the Guru, eternal peace is obtained, by those whom the Lord inspires to obey the Hukam of His Command. ||7||

lines.gurmukhi: ਸੁਇਨਾ ਰੁਪਾ ਸਭ ਧਾਤੁ ਹੈ ਮਾਟੀ ਰਲਿ ਜਾਈ ॥
lines.translit: sueinaa rupaa sabh dhaat hai maattee ral jaaee |
lines.english: Gold and silver, and all metals, mix with dust in the end

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਨਾਮਿ ਰਤੇ ਸੇ ਨਿਰਮਲੇ ਸਾਚੈ ਰਹੇ ਸਮਾਈ ॥੮॥੫॥
lines.translit: naanak naam rate se niramale saachai rahe samaaee |8|5|
lines.english: O Nanak, those who are attuned to the Naam are immaculate and pure; they remain merged in the Truth. ||8||5||

lines.gurmukhi: ਬਿਨੁ ਨਾਵੈ ਨਾਲਿ ਨ ਚਲਈ ਸਤਿਗੁਰਿ ਬੂਝ ਬੁਝਾਈ ॥
lines.translit: bin naavai naal na chalee satigur boojh bujhaaee |
lines.english: Without the Name, nothing goes along with you; the True Guru has imparted this understanding.

lines.gurmukhi: ਮਾਰੂ ਮਹਲਾ ੧ ॥
lines.translit: maaroo mahalaa 1 |
lines.english: Maaroo, First Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਹੁਕਮੁ ਭਇਆ ਰਹਣਾ ਨਹੀ ਧੁਰਿ ਫਾਟੇ ਚੀਰੈ ॥
lines.translit: hukam bheaa rahanaa nahee dhur faatte cheerai |
lines.english: The Order is issued, and he cannot remain; the permit to stay has been torn up.

lines.gurmukhi: ਏਹੁ ਮਨੁ ਅਵਗਣਿ ਬਾਧਿਆ ਸਹੁ ਦੇਹ ਸਰੀਰੈ ॥
lines.translit: ehu man avagan baadhiaa sahu deh sareerai |
lines.english: This mind is tied to its faults; it suffers terrible pain in its body.

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਿਉ ਰਹੀਐ ਉਠਿ ਚਲਣਾ ਬੁਝੁ ਸਬਦ ਬੀਚਾਰਾ ॥
lines.translit: kiau raheeai utth chalanaa bujh sabad beechaaraa |
lines.english: How can he stay here? He must get up and depart. Contemplate the Word of the Shabad, and understand this.

lines.gurmukhi: ਪੂਰੈ ਗੁਰਿ ਬਖਸਾਈਅਹਿ ਸਭਿ ਗੁਨਹ ਫਕੀਰੈ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: poorai gur bakhasaaeeeh sabh gunah fakeerai |1|
lines.english: The Perfect Guru forgives all the mistakes of the beggar at His Door. ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਿਉ ਤੂ ਰਾਖਹਿ ਤਿਉ ਰਹਾ ਜੋ ਦੇਹਿ ਸੁ ਖਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: jiau too raakheh tiau rahaa jo dehi su khaau |
lines.english: As You keep me, I remain; whatever You give me, I eat.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਿਸੁ ਤੂ ਮੇਲਹਿ ਸੋ ਮਿਲੈ ਧੁਰਿ ਹੁਕਮੁ ਅਪਾਰਾ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: jis too meleh so milai dhur hukam apaaraa |1| rahaau |
lines.english: He alone is united, whom You, O Lord, unite. Such is the Primal Command of the Infinite Lord. ||1||Pause||

lines.gurmukhi: ਮੇਰੇ ਠਾਕੁਰ ਹਥਿ ਵਡਿਆਈਆ ਮੇਲਹਿ ਮਨਿ ਚਾਉ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: mere tthaakur hath vaddiaaeea meleh man chaau |2|
lines.english: All glorious greatness rests in the hands of my Lord and Master; my mind yearns to unite with You. ||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਿਉ ਤੂ ਚਲਾਵਹਿ ਤਿਉ ਚਲਾ ਮੁਖਿ ਅੰਮ੍ਰਿਤ ਨਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: jiau too chalaaveh tiau chalaa mukh amrit naau |
lines.english: As You lead me, I follow, with the Ambrosial Name in my mouth.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਿਨਿ ਕੀਆ ਸੋ ਮਨਿ ਵਸੈ ਮੈ ਅਵਰੁ ਨ ਕੋਈ ॥
lines.translit: jin keea so man vasai mai avar na koee |
lines.english: The One who created me, abides within my mind; there is no other at all.

lines.gurmukhi: ਕੀਤਾ ਕਿਆ ਸਾਲਾਹੀਐ ਕਰਿ ਦੇਖੈ ਸੋਈ ॥
lines.translit: keetaa kiaa saalaaheeai kar dekhai soee |
lines.english: Why should anyone praise any other created being? That Lord acts and sees.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸੋ ਸਾਚਾ ਸਾਲਾਹੀਐ ਸਾਚੀ ਪਤਿ ਹੋਈ ॥੩॥
lines.translit: so saachaa saalaaheeai saachee pat hoee |3|
lines.english: So praise that True Lord, and you shall be blessed with true honor. ||3||

lines.gurmukhi: ਪੰਡਿਤੁ ਪੜਿ ਨ ਪਹੁਚਈ ਬਹੁ ਆਲ ਜੰਜਾਲਾ ॥
lines.translit: panddit parr na pahuchee bahu aal janjaalaa |
lines.english: The Pandit, the religious scholar, reads, but does not reach the Lord; he is totally entangled in worldly affairs.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਿਨ ਕੀ ਲੇਖੈ ਪਤਿ ਪਵੈ ਸੇ ਪੂਰੇ ਭਾਈ ॥
lines.translit: jin kee lekhai pat pavai se poore bhaaee |
lines.english: They alone are perfect, O Siblings of Destiny, whose honor is certified.

lines.gurmukhi: ਵਿਛੋੜਾ ਭਉ ਵੀਸਰੈ ਪੂਰਾ ਰਖਵਾਲਾ ॥੪॥
lines.translit: vichhorraa bhau veesarai pooraa rakhavaalaa |4|
lines.english: One who is protected by the Perfect Lord, forgets separation and fear. ||4||

lines.gurmukhi: ਪਾਪ ਪੁੰਨ ਦੁਇ ਸੰਗਮੇ ਖੁਧਿਆ ਜਮਕਾਲਾ ॥
lines.translit: paap pun due sangame khudhiaa jamakaalaa |
lines.english: He keeps the company of both virtue and vice, tormented by hunger and the Messenger of Death.

lines.gurmukhi: ਖਾਰ ਸਮੁਦ੍ਰੁ ਢੰਢੋਲੀਐ ਇਕੁ ਮਣੀਆ ਪਾਵੈ ॥
lines.translit: khaar samudru dtandtoleeai ik maneea paavai |
lines.english: Searching the salty sea, one finds the pearl.

lines.gurmukhi: ਦੇਦੇ ਤੋਟਿ ਨ ਆਵਈ ਲੈ ਲੈ ਥਕਿ ਪਾਈ ॥੫॥
lines.translit: dede tott na aavee lai lai thak paaee |5|
lines.english: His gifts never run short, although those who receive may grow weary of receiving. ||5||

lines.gurmukhi: ਪੂਰੇ ਪੂਰੀ ਮਤਿ ਹੈ ਸਚੀ ਵਡਿਆਈ ॥
lines.translit: poore pooree mat hai sachee vaddiaaee |
lines.english: Perfect is the intellect of the Perfect Lord. True is His glorious greatness.

lines.gurmukhi: ਦੁਇ ਦਿਨ ਚਾਰਿ ਸੁਹਾਵਣਾ ਮਾਟੀ ਤਿਸੁ ਖਾਵੈ ॥
lines.translit: due din chaar suhaavanaa maattee tis khaavai |
lines.english: It looks beautiful for a few days, but in the end, it is eaten away by dust.

lines.gurmukhi: ਗੁਰੁ ਸਾਗਰੁ ਸਤਿ ਸੇਵੀਐ ਦੇ ਤੋਟਿ ਨ ਆਵੈ ॥੬॥
lines.translit: gur saagar sat seveeai de tott na aavai |6|
lines.english: If one serves the Guru, the ocean of Truth, the gifts one receives never run short. ||6||

lines.gurmukhi: ਮੈਲਾ ਊਜਲੁ ਤਾ ਥੀਐ ਪਾਰਸ ਸੰਗਿ ਭੀਜੈ ॥
lines.translit: mailaa aoojal taa theeai paaras sang bheejai |
lines.english: The filthy become pure, when they meet with the Guru, the Philosopher's Stone.

lines.gurmukhi: ਮੇਰੇ ਪ੍ਰਭ ਭਾਵਨਿ ਸੇ ਊਜਲੇ ਸਭ ਮੈਲੁ ਭਰੀਜੈ ॥
lines.translit: mere prabh bhaavan se aoojale sabh mail bhareejai |
lines.english: They alone are pure, who are pleasing to my God; all others are soiled with filth.

lines.gurmukhi: ਪੂਛਉ ਬੇਦ ਪੜੰਤਿਆ ਮੂਠੀ ਵਿਣੁ ਮਾਨੇ ॥
lines.translit: poochhau bed parrantiaa mootthee vin maane |
lines.english: Go and ask the readers of the Vedas; without faith, the world is cheated.

lines.gurmukhi: ਭੇਖੀ ਹਾਥ ਨ ਲਭਈ ਤੀਰਥਿ ਨਹੀ ਦਾਨੇ ॥
lines.translit: bhekhee haath na labhee teerath nahee daane |
lines.english: Wearing religious robes, the Lord is not obtained, nor is He obtained by giving donations at sacred shrines of pilgrimage.

lines.gurmukhi: ਵੰਨੀ ਸਾਚੇ ਲਾਲ ਕੀ ਕਿਨਿ ਕੀਮਤਿ ਕੀਜੈ ॥੭॥
lines.translit: vanee saache laal kee kin keemat keejai |7|
lines.english: Who can estimate the value of the color of the true jewel? ||7||

lines.gurmukhi: ਮਾਰੂ ਮਹਲਾ ੧ ॥
lines.translit: maaroo mahalaa 1 |
lines.english: Maaroo, Fifth Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਕੀਮਤਿ ਸੋ ਕਰੇ ਪੂਰਾ ਗੁਰੁ ਗਿਆਨੇ ॥੮॥੬॥
lines.translit: naanak keemat so kare pooraa gur giaane |8|6|
lines.english: O Nanak, he alone values the jewel, who is blessed with the spiritual wisdom of the Perfect Guru. ||8||6||

lines.gurmukhi: ਗ੍ਰਿਹ ਧਰਮੁ ਗਵਾਏ ਸਤਿਗੁਰੁ ਨ ਭੇਟੈ ਦੁਰਮਤਿ ਘੂਮਨ ਘੇਰੈ ॥
lines.translit: grih dharam gavaae satigur na bhettai duramat ghooman gherai |
lines.english: He neglects his household duties, and does not meet with the True Guru; he is caught in the whirlpool of evil-mindedness.

lines.gurmukhi: ਮਨਮੁਖੁ ਲਹਰਿ ਘਰੁ ਤਜਿ ਵਿਗੂਚੈ ਅਵਰਾ ਕੇ ਘਰ ਹੇਰੈ ॥
lines.translit: manamukh lehar ghar taj vigoochai avaraa ke ghar herai |
lines.english: The self-willed manmukh, in a fit of passion, abandons his home, and is ruined; then, he spies on the homes of others.

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਾਚੀ ਪਿੰਡੀ ਸਬਦੁ ਨ ਚੀਨੈ ਉਦਰੁ ਭਰੈ ਜੈਸੇ ਢੋਰੈ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: kaachee pinddee sabad na cheenai udar bharai jaise dtorai |1|
lines.english: His perishable body does not remember the Word of the Shabad; like a beast, he fills his belly. ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਦਿਸੰਤਰੁ ਭਵੈ ਪਾਠ ਪੜਿ ਥਾਕਾ ਤ੍ਰਿਸਨਾ ਹੋਇ ਵਧੇਰੈ ॥
lines.translit: disantar bhavai paatth parr thaakaa trisanaa hoe vadherai |
lines.english: Wandering in foreign lands and reading scriptures, he grows weary, and his thirsty desires only increase.

lines.gurmukhi: ਗੁਰ ਕੈ ਸਬਦਿ ਏਕ ਲਿਵ ਲਾਗੀ ਤੇਰੈ ਨਾਮਿ ਰਤੇ ਤ੍ਰਿਪਤਾਸੀ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: gur kai sabad ek liv laagee terai naam rate tripataasee |1| rahaau |
lines.english: Through the Word of the Guru's Shabad, he is to enshrine love for the One Lord. Imbued with Your Name, Lord, he remains satisfied and fulfilled. ||1||Pause||

lines.gurmukhi: ਬਾਬਾ ਐਸੀ ਰਵਤ ਰਵੈ ਸੰਨਿਆਸੀ ॥
lines.translit: baabaa aisee ravat ravai saniaasee |
lines.english: O Baba, this is the way of life of the Sannyaasi, the renunciate.

lines.gurmukhi: ਘੋਲੀ ਗੇਰੂ ਰੰਗੁ ਚੜਾਇਆ ਵਸਤ੍ਰ ਭੇਖ ਭੇਖਾਰੀ ॥
lines.translit: gholee geroo rang charraaeaa vasatr bhekh bhekhaaree |
lines.english: He dyes his robes with saffron dye, and wearing these robes, he goes out begging.

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਾਪੜ ਫਾਰਿ ਬਨਾਈ ਖਿੰਥਾ ਝੋਲੀ ਮਾਇਆਧਾਰੀ ॥
lines.translit: kaaparr faar banaaee khinthaa jholee maaeaadhaaree |
lines.english: Tearing his robes, he makes a patched coat, and puts the money in his wallet.

lines.gurmukhi: ਭਰਮਿ ਭੁਲਾਣਾ ਸਬਦੁ ਨ ਚੀਨੈ ਜੂਐ ਬਾਜੀ ਹਾਰੀ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: bharam bhulaanaa sabad na cheenai jooai baajee haaree |2|
lines.english: He is deluded by doubt, and does not remember the Word of the Shabad. He loses his life in the gamble. ||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਘਰਿ ਘਰਿ ਮਾਗੈ ਜਗੁ ਪਰਬੋਧੈ ਮਨਿ ਅੰਧੈ ਪਤਿ ਹਾਰੀ ॥
lines.translit: ghar ghar maagai jag parabodhai man andhai pat haaree |
lines.english: From house to house he goes begging, and tries to teach the world; but his mind is blind, and so he loses his honor.
